headline: National Cybersecurity Act Passed With Mandatory Reporting
context: Parliament passed National Cybersecurity Act in January 2026 mandating breach reporting within 72 hours for all critical sectors.

cards.big: Cybersecurity Act mandates breach reporting within 72 hours from 2026
cards.sub: Law covers 11 critical sectors including finance healthcare and energy

cards.big: Failure to report carries maximum RM10 million fine or 5 years imprisonment
cards.sub: All critical infrastructure operators must conduct annual security audits

cards.big: NACSA empowered to order remediation measures for identified vulnerabilities
cards.sub: Estimated compliance cost of RM2.8 billion across all affected organizations

cards.big: SMEs in critical supply chains face disproportionate compliance burden
cards.sub: Government allocated RM180 million to support SME cybersecurity upgrades

cards.big: Cybersecurity law tests whether mandatory reporting improves security or just increases compliance costs
Mandatory cyber incident reporting changes the calculus. Disclosure obligations create accountability that voluntary norms cannot.
